title: phleum alpinum subsp. commutatum
intro: Phleum alpinum subsp. commutatum, also known as the Alpine Timothy grass, is a subspecies of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Phleum alpinum subsp. commutatum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used internally to treat digestive issues and externally to promote wound healing.
